seo.title: Building Hybrid search (keyword + vector) with Pinecone a...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Hybrid search (keyword + vector) with Pinecone and Weaviate.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to implementing RAG systems, focusing on production considerations for developers working with knowledge bases, enterprise data, and AI-powered search tools. Each step includes actionable implementation details and mitigation strategies for common challenges.

steps.title: Choose and configure vector database
steps.explanation: Select a vector database that matches your scale and latency requirements. Configure indexes with appropriate dimensionality and metric type (e.g., cosine similarity).

code.content:
import pinecone
pinecone.init(api_key='YOUR_API_KEY')
pinecone.create_index(name='rag-index', dimension=768, metric='cosine')

pitfalls: Using incorrect dimensionality for embedding model
Neglecting to set proper index metric type

steps.title: Implement document preprocessing pipeline
steps.explanation: Split documents into optimal chunks (512-1024 tokens) with 20-30% overlap. Use LangChain's RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter for consistent results.

code.content:
from langchain.text_splitter import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter
splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(chunk_size=512, chunk_overlap=64)

pitfalls: Using fixed chunk sizes without content analysis
Ignoring semantic boundaries in text splitting

steps.title: Generate embeddings with cost controls
steps.explanation: Use efficient embedding models (e.g., text-embedding-ada-002) and implement batch processing. Monitor API usage limits and implement rate limiting.
pitfalls: Failing to implement batch processing for large datasets

steps.title: Implement hybrid search strategy
steps.explanation: Combine keyword matching (BM25) with vector similarity search. Use Weaviate's hybrid search or Pinecone's filter capabilities for multi-criteria queries.

code.content:
query = 'system architecture'
results = index.query(vector=embedding, filter={'text': {'$contains': query}})

pitfalls: Over-relying on vector similarity without keyword filters
Ignoring case sensitivity in text filters

steps.title: Integrate with LLM for answer generation
steps.explanation: Use LangChain's VectorDBQAChain or LlamaIndex's QueryEngine to combine retrieved documents with LLM prompts. Include strict prompt engineering to reduce hallucinations.

code.content:
from langchain.chains import VectorDBQAChain
chain = VectorDBQAChain.from_chain_type(llm, vectorstore=index)

pitfalls: Not including retrieved documents in LLM prompt
Using default LLM parameters without fine-tuning

steps.title: Implement reranking for precision
steps.explanation: Use a cross-encoder model (e.g., BERT) to re-rank retrieved documents. Prioritize relevance over raw similarity scores.

code.content:
from sentence_transformers import CrossEncoder
cross_encoder = CrossEncoder('cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2')
scores = cross_encoder.predict([(query, doc) for doc in retrieved_docs])

pitfalls: Using dense models without proper hardware acceleration
Not normalizing reranking scores for comparison

content.conclusion: By following this implementation sequence, developers can build reliable RAG systems that balance accuracy, cost, and latency. Focus on iterative testing of chunk sizes, embedding strategies, and reranking approaches to optimize for specific use cases.
